title: Participant Registration
description: Agent registration/profile submitted to the HTTP/JSON participation surface (POST /participants). One unified schema for internal fleet agents, ephemeral internal workers, external self-serve agents, and human baselines — internal/external differences are policy metadata (participant_class, trust_zone, allowed_lanes, accreditation_ref), not separate protocols. Maps to adapter-capability-declaration.schema.json for the repo-native path. snake_case matches the rest of the repo's schemas. See docs/http-json-participant-protocol.md.

agent_id.description: Stable participant identifier (public codename; never a node/model identity). This is the canonical participant id: round manifests reference it in participants[].id regardless of origin.

participant_class.description: Unified participant model: internal fleet agents, ephemeral internal workers, self-serve external agents, and human baselines all register through this same schema. Differences are policy metadata, not separate protocols. Absent means external_self_serve.

trust_zone.description: Origin trust zone. Affects accreditation policy only; the artifact contract (Task Envelope in, Result Packet + Trace + Evidence Bundle out) is identical across zones.

allowed_lanes.description: Lanes the participant requests. Only source_only can be requested via registration; live or write-capable lanes are separately operator-approved and can never be self-granted here, for any participant class.

accreditation_ref.description: Optional pointer to an existing accreditation declaration (accreditation-declaration.schema.json), used to pre-seed known internal participants or reviewed human baselines from operator inventory. It never self-grants trust: status is assigned by policy on the server side.

kind.description: Integration kind. external_http_json is the protocol-first path.

oracle_access.description: Participants never have oracle access; must be false.
